_about: Lifecycle for seed topics themselves (candidate -> active -> paused -> rejected) — separate from cherry_category (content-type tags in source_registry_template.json) and from per-topic source lists (community_strategy_db.json). Written by Stage 1E (agents/stage1/1E_category_discovery.md). On promotion to active, the category_id is appended to SEED_TOPICS in pipeline.config.sh and 1A/1A-community pick it up on their next run. See SEEDING_STRATEGY.md.

categories.label: Agent governance, access-control & audit-trail tooling

evidence.note: Enterprise pilots repeatedly stalling on missing audit trails, access controls, and governance/orchestration design, not on agent capability itself
MCP-specific enterprise adoption blockers: identity/provisioning, anonymous client registration risk, permissions/SSO

categories.reviewer_notes: Distinct from 'agent'/'mcp' capability discussion — this is the governance/compliance layer blocking enterprise deployment of both. 6 total independent occurrences across 2 shards, different domains.

categories.label: Agent/LLM observability and evaluation tooling (vendor category)

evidence.note: Named vendor tooling (LangSmith, Arize, last9) for production agent monitoring with agent-specific metrics
Prompt/LLM evaluation frameworks: golden datasets, LLM-as-judge scoring, rubric-based regression testing

categories.reviewer_notes: 5 independent occurrences across 2 shards (agent + prompt), all distinct vendors/domains. Recurring named-tool category (LangSmith, Arize, Braintrust, Maxim, last9) rather than generic agent/prompt discussion.

categories.label: Enterprise AI literacy / workforce enablement & training programs

evidence.note: AI Fluency Index measuring observable behaviors of AI-fluency development across conversations
Structured enterprise training programs and courses on organizational AI/skills adoption, distinct from a 'skill' as a technical artifact
Building AI fluency broadly and preparing leaders/teams through organizational change

categories.reviewer_notes: 4 independent occurrences across 3 shards (agent, skills, AX cases) and 4 distinct domains. Distinct from 'skills' (technical Claude Skills artifacts) — this is workforce/organizational change-management content.
